response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with 15+ years of experience, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The most disqualifying gap is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, his experience is concentrated in web/SaaS development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) rather than embedded C programming, and he shows no demonstrated expertise in the security and networking domains central to this position — no MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, L2/L3 networking, or switch chip knowledge. His C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in personal projects is minimal relative to what this role demands.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems
Linux familiarity evidenced through multiple projects and roles
Strong collaborative track record across cross-functional teams (hardware, QA, DevOps)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this position
No experience with embedded networking security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 protocols, switch chips)
C programming exposure is shallow and incidental; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js/web stack, not systems-level embedded C
